steps.title: Compute discriminant and apply quadratic formula
Identify the greater solution
steps.content:
$$\frac{\sqrt{61}}{6}>0$$
Thus $$x_1=-\frac{7}{6}+\frac{\sqrt{61}}{6}$$ is greater than $$x_2=-\frac{7}{6}-\frac{\sqrt{61}}{6}$$.
The greatest solution is $$-\frac{7}{6}+\frac{\sqrt{61}}{6}$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
